parentQuestion: What is the transparent anterior portion of the fibrous tunic that refracts light entering the eye?

scaffolds.q: What are the three tunics (layers) of the eye from outside to inside?
options: Fibrous tunic, vascular tunic (uvea), neural tunic (retina)
scaffolds.explain: The eye has THREE TUNICS: FIBROUS (outer: sclera + cornea), VASCULAR/UVEA (middle: choroid + ciliary body + iris), and NEURAL (inner: retina).

wrongExplains.0: Cornea, lens, and retina are structures but not the three tunic layers.
wrongExplains.2: These are fluids and an opening — not tissue layers.
wrongExplains.3: Sclera and choroid are parts of two tunics; the optic nerve exits the eye, it's not a tunic.

options: To bend light as it passes from one medium to another
scaffolds.explain: REFRACTION is the BENDING of light when it passes between materials with different optical densities (like air to cornea). This bending focuses light on the retina.

wrongExplains.0: Absorption removes light; refraction bends it without removing it.
wrongExplains.2: Reflection bounces light back; refraction bends it through the material.
wrongExplains.3: Blocking is what opaque materials do; the cornea is transparent and bends light through.

scaffolds.q: The fibrous tunic has two parts. The opaque white portion is the sclera. What is the transparent anterior portion?
scaffolds.explain: The CORNEA is the transparent anterior part of the fibrous tunic. The sclera (white of the eye) is the opaque posterior part. Together they form the eye's outer wall.

wrongExplains.0: The iris is part of the VASCULAR tunic (uvea), not the fibrous tunic.
wrongExplains.1: The pupil is an opening (hole), not a tissue structure.
wrongExplains.3: The lens is internal, behind the iris — not part of the outer fibrous tunic.

scaffolds.q: Why is the cornea transparent while the sclera is opaque?
options: The cornea has more blood vessels
The cornea has precisely arranged collagen fibers and is avascular
The cornea is made of different tissue than the sclera
scaffolds.explain: The cornea is transparent because its collagen fibers are PRECISELY ARRANGED and it's AVASCULAR (no blood vessels). The sclera's irregularly arranged fibers scatter light, making it opaque.

wrongExplains.0: The opposite is true — the cornea has NO blood vessels (avascular), which helps it stay clear.
wrongExplains.1: Both have collagen; it's the ARRANGEMENT that differs.
wrongExplains.3: Both are made of collagen-rich connective tissue. The structural arrangement differs, not the tissue type.

scaffolds.q: Which provides more refractive power — the cornea or the lens?
options: The cornea provides more (about 2/3 of total)
scaffolds.explain: The CORNEA provides about 2/3 (~43 diopters) of the eye's refractive power! The lens adds variable focus (~15-20 diopters). Light bends most at the air-cornea interface.

wrongExplains.0: The lens is adjustable but provides less total refractive power than the fixed cornea.
wrongExplains.1: The cornea provides roughly double the lens's refractive power.
wrongExplains.3: Both definitely refract light — that's how we focus images on the retina.
